name: Isavuconazole
executiveSummary: IV triazole (Cresemba) for invasive aspergillosis and mucormycosis; requires an in-line filter.
indications:
## Indications

- Invasive aspergillosis.
- Mucormycosis according to prescription.

## Precautions

- Requires a 0.2–1.2 μm in-line filter.
- Interactions with CYP3A4 inhibitors/inducers.

> Adjust according to institutional protocols and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: Vial with lyophilized powder containing 200 mg of isavuconazonium sulfate (equiv. to 186 mg of isavuconazole) (Cresemba).
adulto.reconstitution: 5 mL of sterile water (SW). Concentration: 40 mg/mL of isavuconazonium sulfate. Shake gently.
adulto.diluent: 200 mg in 250 mL of normal saline (NS) or 5% dextrose (D5W).
adulto.administration: Direct IV: No. Intermittent IV: Yes. Dilute in 250 mL of NS or D5W and infuse over 60 min via an in-line filter (0.2–1.2 μm). Do not use a syringe.
adulto.notes: Requires a 0.2–1.2 μm in-line filter during infusion. Do not administer as a bolus. Loading dose: 200 mg every 8 h × 6 doses (48 h); maintenance: 200 mg/day. Indicated for invasive aspergillosis and mucormycosis. Interactions with CYP3A4 inhibitors/inducers.

pediatrico.dose: Regimens under pediatric infectious disease supervision.

stability:
## Stability

- Reconstituted (in vial): 1 h at room temperature.
- Diluted solution (to be administered): 6 h at room temperature, 24 h refrigerated.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

- Nausea, diarrhea, elevated transaminases, QT prolongation.

bibliography.citation: Infectious Diseases Service, Infection Prevention and Control. UCIP 2026 — Dilution and stability guide.
Infectious Diseases Society of America (IDSA). Clinical practice guidelines.
ANMAT. Drug information and authorized prescribing information in Argentina.
